message:
I've been slowly working through my home prep checklist, and fire safety is one of those things that feels overwhelming until you actually start. I just found this straightforward resource from Ready.gov that breaks down the practical stuff—escape planning, smoke alarms, that kind of thing—without making you feel like you need to panic. Turns out a lot of it is just thoughtful planning, not expensive overhauls.

What's one fire safety thing you've already got handled in your home?
